title: Air-fryer salmon fillet
subtitle: Crisp-skinned fillet cooked in under 10 minutes
excerpt: Skin-on salmon fillets cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 8 to 10 minutes. The skin crisps against the basket base in the same way it would in a very hot frying pan, without the oil spatter. The flesh stays moist at 55 to 60°C internal temperature.
sourceNotes: Salmon is the most consumed fish in Britain and one of the most commonly air-fried proteins. The air fryer suits salmon because it replicates high-dry-heat cooking without requiring much oil or producing spatter. Atlantic salmon farmed in Scotland and Norway dominates the British market; wild Pacific salmon is available but far less common in supermarkets.

content.text: Pat the skin thoroughly dry before cooking. Moisture on the skin surface creates steam, which prevents crisping. Use several sheets of kitchen paper and press firmly. The skin should feel dry to the touch, not just less wet. This single step is the difference between properly crisp skin and a soft, pale finish.

Pat the {{salmon-fillet}} fillets very dry with kitchen paper, especially on the skin side. Brush the flesh side with {{olive-oil}} and season both sides with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Place the fillets skin-side down in the basket. Cook at 200°C for 8 minutes for a translucent centre (55 to 57°C internal) or 10 minutes for fully opaque throughout (60 to 63°C). Do not flip. Rest for 1 minute. Serve with a squeeze of {{lemon}}.

items.symptom: Skin soft and pale after cooking rather than crisp
items.cause: Skin not dried thoroughly enough, or placed flesh-side down
items.fix: Skin must go down. Pat again with fresh kitchen paper if in doubt. Some fillets have a thin layer of moisture under the skin; score it lightly with a sharp knife to help steam escape

items.symptom: White albumin seeping from the flesh during cooking
items.cause: Temperature too high or fish overcooked
items.fix: White albumin is harmless but indicates the flesh is contracting sharply and past the point of optimal moistness. Reduce to 190°C and check at 7 minutes

items.cause: Basket not preheated or basket not clean
items.fix: Always preheat the air fryer before adding the fish. A light spray of oil on the basket also helps. If the fillet tears when lifting, give it another minute: it usually releases once the skin has fully crisped

content.text: Air-fryer salmon is a fast weeknight protein. Serve with new potatoes and tenderstem broccoli, over egg-fried rice, or flaked into a pasta salad. The 10-minute cook time includes no preparation beyond drying the fillets, making it one of the quickest hot meals the air fryer produces.
